name: Signature Verification (crypto.js update)
description: Add signature verification to existing `static/js/crypto.js` and export base64 helpers. Minimal, targeted changes: (1) Export existing module-scoped `base64Encode` and `base64Decode` functions by adding `export` keyword. (2) Add new `verifySignature(message, signature, publicKey)` function that decodes base64 inputs, encodes message via TextEncoder, calls `window.nacl.sign.detached.verify`, and returns a boolean. Entire function is wrapped in try/catch returning false on any exception (fail-closed). Channel messages skip signature verification because `from.pubKey` is removed for pseudonymity — the integration layer must enforce this skip. This component does NOT modify any existing function signatures.

types.name: Base64String
types.description: A string containing valid base64-encoded data. Uses `window.base64js` for encoding/decoding.

types.name: Ed25519PublicKeyBase64
types.description: A base64-encoded Ed25519 public key (32 bytes decoded). Used with TweetNaCl `nacl.sign.detached.verify`.

types.name: Ed25519SignatureBase64
types.description: A base64-encoded Ed25519 detached signature (64 bytes decoded). Produced by `nacl.sign.detached`.

types.name: VerificationResult

fields.name: valid
fields.description: True if and only if nacl.sign.detached.verify returned true with correctly decoded inputs. False in all other cases including exceptions.

types.description: Conceptual result of verifySignature — always a plain boolean. True means the signature is valid for the given message and public key. False means invalid, malformed, or any error occurred (fail-closed).

functions.name: base64Encode
functions.description: Encode a Uint8Array to a base64 string using `window.base64js.fromByteArray`. This is an EXISTING module-scoped function that is being promoted to an export by adding the `export` keyword. No functional change. No signature change.

inputs.name: uint8Array
inputs.description: Raw bytes to encode as base64

error_cases.name: invalid_input_type
error_cases.condition: uint8Array is not a Uint8Array instance

error_cases.name: missing_base64js_global
error_cases.condition: window.base64js is undefined or does not have fromByteArray method

preconditions: window.base64js global is available and provides fromByteArray function
uint8Array is a valid Uint8Array instance
postconditions: Returned string is valid base64 encoding of the input bytes
base64Decode(base64Encode(input)) produces a Uint8Array with identical content to input

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function — reads window.base64js global but has no side effects

functions.name: base64Decode
functions.description: Decode a base64 string to a Uint8Array using `window.base64js.toByteArray`. This is an EXISTING module-scoped function that is being promoted to an export by adding the `export` keyword. No functional change. No signature change.

validators.error_message: Input must be a valid base64 string

error_cases.name: invalid_base64
error_cases.condition: base64String contains characters outside the base64 alphabet or has invalid padding

error_cases.name: missing_base64js_global
error_cases.condition: window.base64js is undefined or does not have toByteArray method

preconditions: window.base64js global is available and provides toByteArray function
base64String is a valid base64-encoded string
postconditions: Returned Uint8Array contains the decoded bytes from the base64 input
base64Encode(base64Decode(validBase64)) returns a string equal to the input (modulo padding normalization)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function — reads window.base64js global but has no side effects

functions.name: verifySignature
functions.description: Verify an Ed25519 detached signature against a message and public key. Decodes base64 signature and publicKey via base64Decode, encodes message via TextEncoder, then calls `window.nacl.sign.detached.verify(messageBytes, signatureBytes, publicKeyBytes)`. Returns boolean. Entire function body is wrapped in try/catch: any exception returns false (fail-closed). MUST NOT be called on channel messages — channel messages have `from.pubKey` removed for pseudonymity; the integration layer must skip verification for channel messages. JSDoc annotated with @param and @returns.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The plaintext message content that was signed. Will be encoded to bytes via `new TextEncoder().encode(message)`.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 detached signature (64 bytes when decoded). Decoded via base64Decode(signature).

validators.error_message: signature must be a base64-encoded string

inputs.name: publicKey
inputs.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 public key (32 bytes when decoded). Decoded via base64Decode(publicKey).

validators.error_message: publicKey must be a base64-encoded string

error_cases.name: malformed_signature_base64
error_cases.condition: signature parameter is not valid base64 (base64Decode throws)

error_data.behavior: Exception caught by try/catch, function returns false

error_cases.name: malformed_publickey_base64
error_cases.condition: publicKey parameter is not valid base64 (base64Decode throws)

error_data.behavior: Exception caught by try/catch, function returns false

error_cases.name: wrong_signature_length
error_cases.condition: Decoded signature is not exactly 64 bytes (nacl.sign.detached.verify throws or returns false)

error_data.behavior: Exception caught by try/catch or nacl returns false

error_cases.name: wrong_publickey_length
error_cases.condition: Decoded publicKey is not exactly 32 bytes (nacl.sign.detached.verify throws or returns false)

error_data.behavior: Exception caught by try/catch or nacl returns false

error_cases.name: signature_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Signature does not match the message content under the given public key

error_data.behavior: nacl.sign.detached.verify returns false, function returns false
Likely signature mismatch or nacl error, caught and returns false
base64Decode produces empty Uint8Array, nacl rejects, caught and returns false
base64Decode produces empty Uint8Array, nacl rejects, caught and returns false

error_cases.name: non_string_input
error_cases.condition: Any parameter is not a string (e.g., null, undefined, number)

error_data.behavior: TypeError caught by try/catch, function returns false

error_cases.name: missing_nacl_global
error_cases.condition: window.nacl is not available at runtime

error_data.behavior: ReferenceError caught by try/catch, function returns false

preconditions: window.nacl global is available and provides nacl.sign.detached.verify function
This function MUST NOT be called on channel messages (channel messages lack from.pubKey)
postconditions: Returns true if and only if: signature base64-decodes to a valid 64-byte Ed25519 signature, publicKey base64-decodes to a valid 32-byte Ed25519 public key, and nacl.sign.detached.verify confirms the signature is valid for the UTF-8 encoded message bytes under that public key
Returns false for any invalid, malformed, or mismatched inputs
Returns false on any thrown exception (fail-closed)
Never throws an exception to the caller
Never logs message content, private keys, or secret key material

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function — reads window.nacl and window.base64js globals but has no side effects. May optionally emit console.warn for debugging but never logs message content or key material.

invariants: verifySignature NEVER throws — all exceptions are caught internally and result in returning false (fail-closed)
verifySignature NEVER logs private keys, secret keys, or decrypted message content per Crypto Safety rules
base64Decode(base64Encode(x)) === x for any valid Uint8Array x (round-trip identity)
Existing function signatures in crypto.js are NOT modified — only new exports and new function added
verifySignature returns true if and only if nacl.sign.detached.verify returns true with correctly decoded inputs
Channel messages MUST NOT be passed to verifySignature — they lack public keys for pseudonymity; this enforcement is the integration layer's responsibility
All exported functions use named exports only (no default exports)
The module file remains under 300 lines
requires: window.nacl global (TweetNaCl.js) must be loaded, providing nacl.sign.detached.verify
window.base64js global (base64-js) must be loaded, providing fromByteArray and toByteArray
TextEncoder available in browser runtime (standard Web API)
Existing static/js/crypto.js module exists with module-scoped base64Encode and base64Decode functions and an exported signMessage function
